hyperedges.label: Craft Visual Direction and Asset Production Flow

nodes.label: Shared Design Laws
nodes.rationale: Apply to every design in both brand and product registers; match implementation complexity to aesthetic vision and enforce absolute bans on AI slop patterns.

nodes.label: AI Slop Test
nodes.rationale: Interfaces fail if someone can immediately identify them as AI-generated; category-reflex and second-order aesthetic-lane checks prevent training-data defaults.

nodes.label: Absolute Design Bans
nodes.rationale: Match-and-refuse patterns including side-stripe borders, gradient text, glassmorphism defaults, hero-metric templates, identical card grids, and modal-first thinking.

nodes.label: No Redesign Core Rule
nodes.rationale: Preserve approved mock visual role and remove presentation chrome that CSS should own; production cleanup not new art direction.

nodes.label: Adaptation vs Scaling
nodes.rationale: Adaptation rethinks the experience for a new context rather than scaling pixels; treating it as scaling is the primary trap.

nodes.label: prefers-reduced-motion Requirement
nodes.rationale: Always provide non-animated alternatives; ignoring reduced motion is an accessibility violation.

nodes.label: Brand Slop Test
nodes.rationale: Brand surfaces fail if visitors ask which AI made it; distinctiveness and POV are required because average is no longer findable.

nodes.label: Extraneous Cognitive Load
nodes.rationale: Mental effort from poor design is pure waste and should be eliminated ruthlessly.

nodes.label: Working Memory Rule
nodes.rationale: Humans hold at most four items in working memory; decision points with more than four options need grouping or progressive disclosure.

nodes.label: OKLCH Color Space
nodes.rationale: OKLCH is perceptually uniform; reduce chroma at lightness extremes and avoid reflex blue or warm-orange hue defaults.

nodes.label: Tinted Neutrals
nodes.rationale: Pure gray feels lifeless; tiny chroma tinted toward the project brand hue creates subconscious cohesion.

nodes.label: Eight Interactive States
nodes.rationale: Every interactive element needs default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, and success states designed; keyboard users never see hover.

nodes.label: Focus Rings with :focus-visible
nodes.rationale: Never outline:none without replacement; show focus ring only for keyboard users via :focus-visible.

nodes.label: CSS Anchor Positioning
nodes.rationale: Tethers overlays to triggers with position:fixed to escape overflow clipping; Chrome 125+ only.

nodes.label: Undo Over Confirmation Dialogs
nodes.rationale: Users click through confirmations mindlessly; undo toast is better except for irreversible actions.

nodes.label: Semantic Z-Index Scale
nodes.rationale: dropdown(100) → sticky(200) → modal-backdrop(300) → modal(400) → toast(500) → tooltip(600) instead of arbitrary 9999.

nodes.label: Spacing System
nodes.rationale: Consistent scale matters more than specific values; use gap over margins and clamp() for fluid spacing.

nodes.label: Flexbox vs Grid Selection
nodes.rationale: Flex for 1D layouts; Grid for 2D page structure; do not default to Grid when Flex suffices.

nodes.label: Identity Lock
nodes.rationale: Extract actual palette, type pairing, layout topology from DESIGN.md or computed styles before planning variants; every variant must read as same brand.

nodes.label: Default vs Departure Mode
nodes.rationale: Default preserves identity (~90% of sessions); departure only when anti-references target current surface or user explicitly asks.

nodes.label: Live Parameters Contract
nodes.rationale: range/steps/toggle params drive CSS vars or data attributes for instant tuning without regeneration.

nodes.label: Carbonize Cleanup
nodes.rationale: After accept, move inline @scope CSS to real stylesheet, bake param values, unwrap variant wrappers before next poll.

nodes.label: 100/300/500 Duration Rule
nodes.rationale: Timing matters more than easing; exit animations ~75% of enter duration.

nodes.label: Premium Motion Materials
nodes.rationale: Transform/opacity are defaults not limits; blur, clip-path, filters allowed when bounded and smooth.

nodes.label: prefers-reduced-motion
nodes.rationale: Required for vestibular accessibility; preserve functional motion like progress indicators.

nodes.label: Time to Value
nodes.rationale: Onboarding gets users to aha moment fast, not teaching everything; teach 20% delivering 80% value.

nodes.label: Core Web Vitals
nodes.rationale: Target LCP<2.5s, INP<200ms, CLS<0.1; measure before optimizing.

nodes.label: Layout Thrashing Avoidance
nodes.rationale: Batch DOM reads then writes to avoid alternating read/write reflows.

nodes.label: Propose Before Building
nodes.rationale: Highest misfire risk command; present 2-3 directions with trade-offs before implementation.

nodes.label: Design System Discovery
nodes.rationale: Polish without alignment is decoration on drift; classify deviations as missing token, one-off, or conceptual misalignment.

nodes.label: Product Register
nodes.rationale: Design serves the task in app UIs; earned familiarity beats strangeness without purpose.

nodes.label: Color Refinement for Quieter UI
nodes.rationale: Reduce saturation, neutral dominance, tinted grays; never gray text on colored backgrounds.

nodes.label: Mobile-First Responsive
nodes.rationale: Base styles for mobile, min-width queries layer complexity; content-driven breakpoints.

nodes.label: Design Brief
nodes.rationale: Structured artifact from discovery interview gating implementation; user must confirm before code.

nodes.label: 4pt Spacing Base
nodes.rationale: 8pt too coarse; 4pt gives 12px granularity between 8 and 16.

nodes.label: Container Queries
nodes.rationale: Components respond to container width not viewport; sidebar cards stay compact automatically.

nodes.label: PRODUCT.md
nodes.rationale: Strategic context: register, users, personality, anti-references; answers who/what/why.

nodes.label: DESIGN.md
nodes.rationale: Visual system tokens following Google Stitch format; answers how it looks.

nodes.label: Register Brand vs Product
nodes.rationale: Brand: design IS product; Product: design serves task; shapes all subsequent commands.

nodes.label: Modular Type Scale
nodes.rationale: Fewer sizes with more contrast; ratios 1.25, 1.333, or 1.5.

nodes.label: Fluid vs Fixed Type Scale
nodes.rationale: Fluid clamp() for marketing headings; fixed rem for app UIs needing spatial predictability.

nodes.label: Button Label Problem
nodes.rationale: Use specific verb+object labels; never OK/Submit/Yes-No.

nodes.label: Error Message Formula
nodes.rationale: Answer what happened, why, and how to fix; never blame the user.

nodes.label: KingGold Dashboard Shopify Embed
nodes.rationale: Full-viewport iframe wrapper embedding kinggold-dashboard-next.vercel.app into Shopify page HTML.

nodes.label: Layers Icon Motif
nodes.rationale: Three stacked rhombus paths matching the Lucide Layers icon shape; used as the KingGold brand mark in the browser tab, echoing layered inventory semantics.

nodes.label: Gold Stroke Color (#ca8a04)
nodes.rationale: Tailwind yellow-600 hex applied as stroke-only rendering (fill none, stroke-width 2) to keep the favicon crisp at small sizes while signaling the KingGold brand palette.

nodes.label: Browser Tab Icon
nodes.rationale: Static SVG asset served from /public for Next.js metadata icons; identifies KingGold Dashboard in browser chrome and bookmarks.
